localization.direction: Locale metadata must support both ltr and rtl; no visible text is embedded in images or SVG assets.

briefingUx.scope: Design only; existing Survival gameplay remains unchanged.
entrySequence: freeze score, timer, difficulty, collisions, and progression
briefingUx.firstVisit: Full briefing is mandatory and includes concept, new rule, tip, demonstration, and acknowledgement.
briefingUx.repeatVisit: Compact reminder offers Continue and Review rule; a future preference may enable quick resume.
briefingUx.safetyInvariant: No score, timer, collision, difficulty, spawn, or progress disadvantage can occur while a briefing is open.
briefingUx.reducedMotion: Replace fades, parallax, pulses, and countdown scaling with instant state changes, a static diagram, and text numerals.

briefings.blockchainConcept: Distributed ledger foundations, node agreement, and carefully labeled historical Pi trust concepts.
briefings.gameplayRule: Only records seen and verified by the required fictional nodes open the next gate.
briefings.playerGoal: Synchronize the three visible node lanes before crossing the validation line.
briefings.newHazard: A diverged record blinks with both an amber outline and a broken-link pattern.
briefings.newOpportunity: A verified-record lane creates a short safe corridor.
briefings.oneSentenceConcept: A distributed ledger uses shared rules so multiple participants can converge on one ordered state.
briefings.oneSentenceGameplayRule: Follow the lane whose record matches all required node checks.
briefings.oneSentenceTip: Match record shape and order; color alone is never the answer.
briefings.demonstrationIdea: Three static ledger strips receive one record; the mismatched strip is rejected, then the two valid strips align.

fr.idea: Plusieurs nœuds vérifient le même registre ordonné.
fr.rule: Suis la voie dont l'enregistrement passe tous les contrôles.
fr.tip: Compare la forme et l'ordre, pas seulement la couleur.

en.idea: Several nodes verify the same ordered ledger.
en.rule: Follow the lane whose record passes every check.
en.tip: Compare shape and order, not color alone.

repeatVisitBriefing.fr: Rappel : choisis l'enregistrement vérifié par tous les nœuds requis.
repeatVisitBriefing.en: Reminder: choose the record verified by every required node.

briefings.reducedMotionVersion: Show three static strips with check or broken-link icons; reveal the valid route without fades or pulses.
accessibilityNotes: Use icon, pattern, and text labels in addition to color.
Keep the player visible but mark it aria-hidden in the non-interactive diagram.
Historical Pi references must carry a visible Historical concept label.
briefings.simplificationNotes: Three fictional nodes teach convergence; the zone is not a current Pi consensus simulator.

briefings.blockchainConcept: Bitcoin-style UTXOs, proof-of-work blocks, and probabilistic confirmation.
briefings.gameplayRule: A whole output capsule can be used once; confirmed block plates become durable floor segments.
briefings.playerGoal: Collect one unspent input, route its change, and remain on the branch gaining confirmations.
briefings.newHazard: Spent-output decoys crack when approached and competing branch plates can fade.
briefings.newOpportunity: Correct change routing creates a shielded floor segment as a gameplay reward only.
briefings.oneSentenceConcept: Bitcoin transactions consume unspent outputs and confidence grows as valid work builds on their block.
briefings.oneSentenceGameplayRule: Use each output once and stay with the branch gaining valid work.
briefings.oneSentenceTip: A spent marker means the capsule cannot be used again.
briefings.demonstrationIdea: One input capsule enters a transaction gate, disappears, and two new output capsules emerge while confirmation rings stack.

fr.idea: Une sortie non dépensée est consommée une seule fois.
fr.rule: Prends une sortie valide et reste sur la branche confirmée.
fr.tip: Le marqueur dépensé interdit toute réutilisation.

en.idea: An unspent output can be consumed only once.
en.rule: Take a valid output and stay on the confirming branch.

repeatVisitBriefing.fr: Rappel : une sortie, une dépense ; surveille les confirmations.
repeatVisitBriefing.en: Reminder: one output, one spend; watch confirmations.

briefings.reducedMotionVersion: Replace branch fades with solid available or crossed-out plates and increment a text confirmation counter.
accessibilityNotes: Spent state uses a crosshatch pattern and text.
Confirmation depth is announced politely and never as a universal guarantee.
All units are fictional.
briefings.simplificationNotes: The runner rule omits Bitcoin scripts, fee policy, and actual confirmation-risk estimates.

briefings.blockchainConcept: Ethereum-style accounts, smart-contract conditions, gas metering, and reversion.
briefings.gameplayRule: Each rule gate costs abstract gas cells; failed conditions return the player to the pre-call lane state.
briefings.playerGoal: Choose a valid contract route that reaches the exit before the abstract gas meter empties.
briefings.newHazard: An out-of-gas gate closes and a revert wave restores the route state.
briefings.newOpportunity: A valid atomic combo opens several gates with one planned action sequence.
briefings.oneSentenceConcept: Smart contracts execute deterministic rules, while gas bounds their computation and state work.
briefings.oneSentenceGameplayRule: Satisfy each rule without exhausting the abstract gas cells.
briefings.oneSentenceTip: Read the gate cost before committing to a branch.
briefings.demonstrationIdea: Two routes display fixed operation costs; the shorter valid route completes while the other reverts at zero.

fr.idea: Un contrat exécute des règles avec un budget de ressources.
fr.rule: Atteins la sortie sans épuiser les cellules de gas fictives.
fr.tip: Lis le coût du portail avant de choisir.

en.idea: A contract runs rules within a resource budget.
en.rule: Reach the exit without exhausting fictional gas cells.
en.tip: Read each gate cost before choosing.

repeatVisitBriefing.fr: Rappel : respecte la règle et garde assez de gas fictif.
repeatVisitBriefing.en: Reminder: satisfy the rule and keep enough fictional gas.

briefings.reducedMotionVersion: Use a static route diagram, numeric costs, and an instant reset label instead of a rollback wave.
accessibilityNotes: Announce remaining cells after each gate.
Do not use flicker for reversion.
State explicitly that cells are not prices or real gas amounts.
briefings.simplificationNotes: Fixed cells teach execution metering, not live fees or full EVM semantics.

briefings.blockchainConcept: Parallel execution, state conflicts, throughput constraints, and congestion.
briefings.gameplayRule: Independent jobs may share parallel lanes; jobs touching the same marked state must run in sequence.
briefings.playerGoal: Use parallel lanes for independent jobs while avoiding write-conflict barriers.
briefings.newHazard: Two jobs with the same write marker create a conflict wall when run together.
briefings.newOpportunity: Three independent jobs generate a temporary synchronized speed lane.
briefings.oneSentenceConcept: Parallel execution speeds independent work but conflicting state access still needs coordination.
briefings.oneSentenceGameplayRule: Run different markers together and serialize matching write markers.
briefings.oneSentenceTip: Same write symbol means conflict, even when lane colors differ.
briefings.demonstrationIdea: Three job cards split across lanes; independent circles finish together while two square-write cards queue.

fr.idea: Les tâches indépendantes avancent en parallèle ; les écritures en conflit attendent.
fr.rule: Regroupe les marqueurs différents et sépare les mêmes écritures.
fr.tip: Le symbole d'écriture révèle le conflit.

en.idea: Independent jobs run in parallel; conflicting writes wait.
en.rule: Group different markers and separate matching writes.
en.tip: The write symbol reveals the conflict.

repeatVisitBriefing.fr: Rappel : parallélise les tâches indépendantes, séquence les conflits.
repeatVisitBriefing.en: Reminder: parallelize independent jobs and sequence conflicts.

briefings.reducedMotionVersion: Show jobs as a static two-column schedule with Conflict or Independent text labels.
accessibilityNotes: Conflict markers combine shape and text.
Speed effects disable under reduced motion.
Do not claim a universal performance figure.
briefings.simplificationNotes: The rule combines a shared dependency lesson; concrete systems discover or declare conflicts differently.

briefings.blockchainConcept: Quorum overlap, network coherence, and deterministic finality — not a stablecoin.
briefings.gameplayRule: A gate locks only after the required overlapping node groups approve the same checkpoint.
briefings.playerGoal: Keep the trust grid connected and cross only checkpoints with a visible finality seal.
briefings.newHazard: Disjoint node groups create a split-grid warning and remove the unsafe gate.
briefings.newOpportunity: A well-overlapped quorum locks a stable safe bridge across the zone.
briefings.oneSentenceConcept: Overlapping quorum choices help a federated network agree on one final checkpoint.
briefings.oneSentenceGameplayRule: Cross only when the groups overlap and the finality seal is present.
briefings.oneSentenceTip: A bright vote is not enough; look for safe overlap.
briefings.demonstrationIdea: Two candidate quorum diagrams appear; the disjoint one splits, while the intersecting one produces a seal.

fr.idea: Des quorums qui se chevauchent soutiennent une décision cohérente.
fr.rule: Traverse seulement avec chevauchement et sceau final.
fr.tip: Un vote lumineux ne suffit pas : vérifie l'intersection.

en.idea: Overlapping quorums support one coherent decision.
en.rule: Cross only with overlap and a finality seal.
en.tip: A bright vote is not enough; check intersection.

repeatVisitBriefing.fr: Rappel : intersection sûre, puis finalité — ce n'est pas un stablecoin.
repeatVisitBriefing.en: Reminder: safe overlap, then finality — this is not a stablecoin.

briefings.reducedMotionVersion: Present two static quorum diagrams and place a text Final or Split badge beside each.
accessibilityNotes: Use solid and dashed group boundaries plus labels.
Keep the stablecoin disclaimer in the accessible description.
Do not equate trust edges with friendship.
briefings.simplificationNotes: The small SCP-inspired graph teaches overlap and finality concepts, not a full ballot protocol or current Pi configuration.

briefings.blockchainConcept: Economic signals, crowd pressure, scarcity cues, and volatile game-state risk are application-layer concerns, not consensus.
briefings.gameplayRule: Signal gates may reverse their fictional bonus state; protocol-valid route markers never change with crowd signals.
briefings.playerGoal: Follow validity markers and treat flashing popularity signals only as optional fictional risk information.
briefings.newHazard: A crowd-arrow bonus can invert before the next gate without changing transaction validity.
briefings.newOpportunity: Ignoring unsupported hype keeps a reliable neutral route open.
briefings.oneSentenceConcept: Market and crowd signals can change quickly, but they do not decide whether a protocol-valid transaction follows consensus rules.
briefings.oneSentenceGameplayRule: Trust validity markers; treat popularity flashes as optional fictional risk.
briefings.demonstrationIdea: A crowd arrow flips direction while the signed-validity seal remains unchanged on the neutral lane.

fr.idea: Les signaux de foule changent ; les règles de validité restent distinctes.
fr.rule: Suis les marqueurs valides, pas les éclats de popularité.
fr.tip: La popularité n'est pas une preuve de protocole.

en.idea: Crowd signals change; validity rules remain separate.
en.rule: Follow valid markers, not popularity flashes.

repeatVisitBriefing.fr: Rappel : le signal économique fictif n'est pas du consensus.
repeatVisitBriefing.en: Reminder: the fictional economic signal is not consensus.

briefings.reducedMotionVersion: Replace flashing arrows with static High uncertainty labels and keep the neutral validity marker fixed.
accessibilityNotes: No flashing, price chart, token name, gain language, or investment framing.
The safe route is identifiable without interpreting emotion or crowd imagery.
All risk and bonus units are fictional gameplay state.
briefings.simplificationNotes: The zone is a proposed media-literacy rule, not a market simulator or technical profile of any meme token.

briefings.blockchainConcept: Zero-knowledge proof, shielded state, limited disclosure, and observation memory.
briefings.gameplayRule: Reveal the proof marker required by a gate while keeping the fictional witness tiles covered.
briefings.playerGoal: Remember the hidden pattern, build a valid proof, and avoid unnecessary disclosure tiles.
briefings.newHazard: Observation beams expose a covered tile when the player chooses an over-disclosing route.
briefings.newOpportunity: A valid proof opens the gate without uncovering the witness board.
briefings.oneSentenceConcept: A zero-knowledge proof can verify a statement while limiting disclosure of its secret witness.
briefings.oneSentenceGameplayRule: Prove the pattern without revealing the covered witness tiles.
briefings.oneSentenceTip: The gate needs the proof, not the secret.
briefings.demonstrationIdea: A covered 2×2 pattern produces a proof prism; the verifier checks it while the cover stays closed.

fr.idea: Une preuve peut vérifier une propriété sans révéler le témoin secret.
fr.rule: Prouve le motif sans découvrir les cases protégées.
fr.tip: Le portail demande la preuve, pas le secret.

en.idea: A proof can verify a property without revealing the secret witness.
en.rule: Prove the pattern without uncovering protected tiles.
en.tip: The gate needs the proof, not the secret.

repeatVisitBriefing.fr: Rappel : montre la preuve minimale, garde le témoin couvert.
repeatVisitBriefing.en: Reminder: show the minimum proof and keep the witness covered.

briefings.reducedMotionVersion: Use a static covered board, a text Proof valid result, and no scanning beams or opacity animation.
accessibilityNotes: Provide a non-memory alternative that keeps the pattern available in an accessible description.
Do not rely on low contrast or blur to represent privacy.
Never claim absolute anonymity.
briefings.simplificationNotes: The proof prism teaches statement/witness separation and does not model Zcash cryptography or imply that every ZK system is private.

briefings.blockchainConcept: Cross-chain messages, rollup batches, proof verification, acknowledgements, and simultaneous rule domains.
briefings.gameplayRule: Each packet must follow its labeled verifier, sequence, destination, and timeout before the multichain exit opens.
briefings.playerGoal: Route source-authenticated packets and settle one rollup batch without replay or timeout.
briefings.newHazard: Portals use different proof rules and duplicate packets trigger replay barriers.
briefings.newOpportunity: A returned acknowledgement stabilizes a portal and clears its storm lane.
briefings.oneSentenceConcept: Cross-chain messages are safe only under their specific source proof, destination, ordering, and timeout rules.
briefings.oneSentenceGameplayRule: Match every packet to its verifier and return acknowledgements before timeout.
briefings.oneSentenceTip: A courier moves data; the verifier establishes authenticity.
briefings.demonstrationIdea: An IBC-style packet takes the light-client gate while a rollup batch takes a validity-proof gate; swapping them fails.

fr.idea: Chaque message inter-chaînes dépend de sa propre preuve et de son ordre.
fr.rule: Associe paquet, vérificateur et accusé avant l'expiration.
fr.tip: Le relais transporte ; le vérificateur authentifie.

en.idea: Each cross-chain message depends on its own proof and order.
en.rule: Match packet, verifier, and acknowledgement before timeout.
en.tip: The relay carries; the verifier authenticates.

repeatVisitBriefing.fr: Rappel : bonne preuve, bon ordre, bon portail, avant l'expiration.
repeatVisitBriefing.en: Reminder: right proof, order, and portal before timeout.

briefings.reducedMotionVersion: Replace storm particles and portal rotation with a static packet table showing source, verifier, sequence, and deadline.
accessibilityNotes: Every portal has a shape and text code.
Timeouts pause with the rest of gameplay during the briefing.
Use a single live-region update per packet result.
briefings.simplificationNotes: The final zone compares security models without treating IBC, XCM, and rollups as interchangeable bridges.
